commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation. "Salvation," in Greek σωτήζιον, the word used by the Septuagint as a rendering of the Hebrew ישוצח, iescua, safety. "Safety" is used by metonomy for "Saviour." By "salvation," then, we are to understand the Saviour Christ, whom the ancient fathers desired to see, but Simeon alone saw, touched, and embraced.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Thy salvation; i.e. the Saviour, whom thou hast sent. (Witham)
